⚠ PDCD1/CD2 ratio uses raw count expression — large absolute values are expected. All three features are min-max normalised before weighting for Sj. fexh = fraction exhausted among CD8; fCD8 = CD8 fraction of all T cells.
High Exhaustion / High PD-1
3 patients
CID44971, CID4515, CID4523
fexh > 0.40 & ratio > 1.0
Increase PD-1 axis density + CD2/adhesion support
Moderate Exhaustion / High PD-1
20 patients
Majority group (77%)
fexh < 0.40, ratio ≥ 1.0
Moderate PD-1 density + synapse stabilisation
Low T Cell Infiltration
3 patients
CID4465, CID4290A, CID4461
fCD8 < 0.15 or fexh = 0
T cell recruitment first; axis optimisation secondary
Clinical Validation — TCGA-BRCA Survival (n=1,214 samples)
Cox Proportional Hazards Models — Real Output
PDCD1/CD2 Ratio
0.47
exp(coef) · coef = −0.75 · SE = 0.26
95% CI: 0.28 – 0.79
z = −2.86 · p < 0.005 · concordance = 0.56
Bulk Exhaustion Score
0.89
exp(coef) · coef = −0.12 · SE = 0.05
95% CI: 0.81 – 0.98
z = −2.43 · p = 0.02 · concordance = 0.55
⚠ Unadjusted exploratory Cox model. No clinical covariates (stage, subtype, purity, grade). HR<1 for PDCD1/CD2 reflects PDCD1 as a surrogate of immune infiltration in mixed-subtype BRCA (beneficial in luminal subtypes). True exhaustion signal visible at single-cell resolution in pure TNBC. n=1,214 · 198 events · 1,016 right-censored.

Bootstrap Stability Analysis — n=200 Cell-Level Resamples
Real Bootstrap Statistics
Mean Spearman ρ
0.905
std = 0.041 · threshold >0.80 ✓
CID44971 Stability
100%
top-3 in all 200 resamples
Procedure: Per-patient cell-level bootstrap (replacement, n=200). DesignPriorityScore recomputed each iteration with identical weights (w1=0.50, w2=0.30, w3=0.20). Spearman ρ between base ranking and each bootstrap ranking computed across all 26 patients. Top-3 frequency = fraction of 200 resamples patient appeared in ranks 1–3.

17 of 26 patients never appeared in top-3 across all 200 resamples.
CID44971 is the most robust engineering candidate in the cohort.
Chowdhury K. · Universität des Saarlandes · ORCID 0009-0007-6319-3769
